articles.title: WHAT DID META BUY? A PERSON? A DATASET? A CAPABILITY?
articles.summary: Meta's $14 billion quasi-acquisition of Scale AI raises questions about what it actually purchased, as Scale missed revenue targets and lost major customers like OpenAI before the deal.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC'S CLAUDE CAN NOW BUILD AI APPS
articles.summary: Anthropic is rolling out a beta feature that lets Claude users build, host, and share AI-powered apps directly within the Claude app without separate API costs. The feature allows Claude to write real code and handle technical tasks, though it has limitations like restricted external API access and no persistent data storage.

articles.title: DON'T KICKOFF A FUNDRAISE IN LATE SUMMER
articles.summary: While fundraising data shows steady deal volume through summer months, the real impact of VCs vacationing appears 1-2 months later as September/October deal volume drops 15-30% from peaks. Launching a fundraise between July 15 and August 31 means slower VC response times, less momentum, and worse terms, so founders should either start early summer or wait until September unless runway demands immediate action.

articles.title: THE GREAT AI TALENT GRAB: THE LATEST 20VC WITH JASON, HARRY, AND RORY
articles.summary: Silicon Valley's AI talent wars signify a major shift, with Meta spending defensively to protect its platform by acquiring AI talent. The success of companies like Harvey and Cluely highlights a strategy of securing market position and customer relationships before perfecting their technology, emphasizing speed over initial product quality. B2B SaaS companies face crucial strategic decisions between protecting existing revenue and embracing new AI-driven business models to thrive in this rapidly evolving landscape.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN: "NO MATTER HOW GREAT YOUR IDEA IS, NO ONE CARES."
articles.summary: Sam Altman tells founders worried about idea theft that everyone is too distracted to steal your concept anyway and proves it by openly sharing YC's exact playbook with potential competitors who never execute on the advice.

articles.title: HOW TO FIND SUPERSTAR TALENT
articles.summary: Marc Lore shares strategies for identifying and hiring top 5% talent, emphasizing consistent company culture and early hiring of a Chief People Officer. He highlights the rarity of "superstars" and the importance of assessing tenure, trajectory, and cultural fit over experience alone. Lore advocates for systematic performance management and transparency as tools for fostering employee engagement and trust.

articles.title: TIRED OF VIRTUAL DESKTOP LAG AND LATENCY ON VOICE/VIDEO CALLS? TRY THIS LOCAL-FIRST ALTERNATIVE
articles.summary: What is local-first? Users work with company data and applications that are running locally and natively on their PC or Mac. Application performance remains 100%. Meet Venn - the #1 Alternative to VDI that secures apps and data on unmanaged devices without remote hosting or virtualization. Get a better user experience, reduce infrastructure cost and complexity and strengthen SOC 2, FINRA, PCI, HIPAA and more.

articles.title: SONA
articles.summary: Your always-on-call AI agent, by the OpenPhone team.

articles.title: CORA
articles.summary: AI Chief of Staff that lives in your inbox.

articles.title: COMPETELY AI AGENT
articles.summary: Competely is an AI agent that instantly finds, analyzes, and tracks your competitors with automated reports and updates.

articles.title: FIVE JOBS
articles.summary: When cognitive work gets automated everything starts looking like trade work - lawyers and electricians will have more in common than lawyers and other professionals. The "gentle singularity" shifts human work toward physical and relational tasks because machines can think better than humans but can't actually be human, making what was once common suddenly scarce and valuable.

articles.title: THEY BUILT THE WEB. THEN LOST IT
articles.summary: CareerBuilder and Monster filed for bankruptcy after their 2024 merger couldn't save two companies that once dominated online job hunting with $8+ billion valuations. The collapse wasn't surprising since LinkedIn took a smarter path by building a professional network instead of just posting job listings while the old giants never evolved beyond resume databases as hiring moved toward specialized platforms.

articles.title: WHEN IS 'GOOD ENOUGH' GOOD ENOUGH?
articles.summary: AI excels at "good enough" work, forcing leaders to distinguish between tasks where AI-generated output suffices (like routine emails) and where human excellence matters (like board presentations). The key framework asks: Does human effort create leverage? Is speed more valuable than quality? What's the risk of getting it wrong? Smart executives will automate low-stakes work to preserve cognitive energy for high-impact decisions while occasionally letting teams over-invest in craftsmanship where it truly matters.

articles.title: YOU WANT TO HATE AI SLOP BUT YOU ABSOLUTELY LOVE IT
articles.summary: A study of 17,000 people found we prefer AI-generated poems over human ones when tested blind but immediately change our opinions once we learn the true authorship.

articles.title: GTM IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: ICONIQ's survey of 205 B2B executives revealed AI-native companies achieve 56% trial-to-paid conversion rates compared to just 32% for traditional SaaS companies while running 38% leaner GTM teams.

articles.title: KALSHI CLOSES $185M ROUND AS RIVAL POLYMARKET REPORTEDLY SEEKS $200M
articles.summary: Kalshi raised $185 million at a $2 billion valuation, led by Paradigm, highlighting growing interest in prediction markets.
